Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). 2026-04-07T07:04:04.1580980Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-07T07:03:31.397710816Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet`\n**Service directory:** `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/`\n**Triggered by:** PR #57844 by @frantran\n\n## What Changed\n\nPR #57844 generated version `1.2.0-beta.2` by upgrading the API version to `2025-08-01-preview`. This added two new resource types to the SDK:\n\n- **`ContainerServiceFleetGate` / `ContainerServiceFleetGateData` / `ContainerServiceFleetGateCollection`** — Gates support for fleet-level upgrade coordination\n- **`ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespace` / `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData` / `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceCollection`** — Managed Namespace support\n\nCombined with features from `1.2.0-beta.1` (AutoUpgrade profiles, FleetMember labels, TargetKubernetesVersion), the package now has substantially more API surface than what the README describes.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- The README **Examples** section contains no inline code samples — it only links to `https://aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples`, which is a generic redirect. There is no package-specific example showing how to create a Fleet, add a member, trigger an update run, create a Gate, or manage a ManagedNamespace.\n- There are **no snippet placeholders** (`C# Snippet:`) in the README that would be populated by `Update-Snippets.ps1`.\n- There is **no `tests/Samples/` directory** with hand-crafted sample test files. The only samples are the auto-generated ones under `tests/Generated/Samples/`, which are not wired into the README.\n- The **Key concepts** section is a one-liner pointing to general SDK docs and says nothing specific to the Fleet service (e.g., what a Fleet is, what Members are, what Update Runs are, what Gates are, what Managed Namespaces are).\n- The new **Gates** and **ManagedNamespace** features added in this and the prior beta are not mentioned or demonstrated anywhere in the README.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Update `README.md`\n\n**File:** `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/README.md`\n**Action:** Edit\n\nReplace the placeholder **Key concepts** section with a concise description of the Fleet service domain model:\n\n```markdown\n## Key concepts\n\n### Fleet\n\nA `Fleet` resource (represented by `ContainerServiceFleetResource`) is the top-level management object that groups AKS member clusters for coordinated lifecycle management.\n\n### Fleet Member\n\nA `ContainerServiceFleetMember` represents an AKS cluster enrolled in the fleet. Members can be labeled for targeted update strategies.\n\n### Update Run\n\nA `ContainerServiceFleetUpdateRun` orchestrates rolling upgrades across member clusters in defined stages and groups.\n\n### Update Strategy\n\nA `FleetUpdateStrategy` defines a reusable template of stages and groups for update runs.\n\n### Auto Upgrade Profile\n\nAn `AutoUpgradeProfile` automates Kubernetes version upgrades across fleet members based on release channel settings.\n\n### Gate\n\nA `ContainerServiceFleetGate` is a pre/post-upgrade checkpoint that can pause an update run until external conditions are satisfied, enabling integration with deployment pipelines.\n\n### Managed Namespace\n\nA `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespace` propagates a Kubernetes namespace (with RBAC, resource quotas, and network policies) across all fleet member clusters from the fleet hub.\n```\n\nReplace the placeholder **Examples** section content with snippet placeholders (see Step 2):\n\n```markdown\n## Examples\n\nThe following examples demonstrate common operations with the Azure Kubernetes Fleet Manager library.\n\n### Create a Fleet\n\n```C# Snippet:CreateFleet\n```\n\n### Add a Fleet Member\n\n```C# Snippet:AddFleetMember\n```\n\n### Start an Update Run\n\n```C# Snippet:CreateUpdateRun\n```\n\n### Create a Gate\n\n```C# Snippet:CreateGate\n```\n\n### Create a Managed Namespace\n\n```C# Snippet:CreateManagedNamespace\n```\n\nFor more examples, see the [Azure SDK for .NET management library samples](https://aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples).\n```\n\n\u003e **Note:** The backtick fences above are abbreviated for clarity. In the actual README, use triple backticks on each opening and closing line as normal markdown code fences.\n\n### Step 2: Create sample test file\n\n**File:** `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/tests/Samples/Sample1_FleetOperations.cs`\n**Action:** Create\n\n```csharp\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\n\nusing System;\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\nusing Azure.Identity;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet.Models;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\nusing NUnit.Framework;\n\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet.Tests.Samples\n{\n    public class Sample1_FleetOperations : SamplesBase\u003cContainerServiceFleetManagementTestEnvironment\u003e\n    {\n        [Test]\n        [SyncOnly]\n        [Ignore(\"Only verifying that the sample builds\")]\n        public async Task CreateFleet()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:CreateFleet\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\n\n            string subscriptionId = \"\u003csubscription-id\u003e\";\n            string resourceGroupName = \"\u003cresource-group-name\u003e\";\n            string fleetName = \"\u003cfleet-name\u003e\";\n\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client\n                .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($\"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\"))\n                .GetResourceGroupAsync(resourceGroupName);\n\n            ContainerServiceFleetCollection fleets = resourceGroup.GetContainerServiceFleets();\n\n            ContainerServiceFleetData fleetData = new ContainerServiceFleetData(AzureLocation.EastUS);\n\n            ArmOperation\u003cContainerServiceFleetResource\u003e operation = await fleets.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\n                WaitUntil.Completed,\n                fleetName,\n                fleetData);\n\n            ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = operation.Value;\n            Console.WriteLine($\"Created Fleet: {fleet.Data.Name}\");\n            #endregion\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        [SyncOnly]\n        [Ignore(\"Only verifying that the sample builds\")]\n        public async Task AddFleetMember()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:AddFleetMember\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\n\n            ResourceIdentifier fleetId = ContainerServiceFleetResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(\n                \"\u003csubscription-id\u003e\",\n                \"\u003cresource-group-name\u003e\",\n                \"\u003cfleet-name\u003e\");\n\n            ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = client.GetContainerServiceFleetResource(fleetId);\n\n            ContainerServiceFleetMemberCollection members = fleet.GetContainerServiceFleetMembers();\n\n            string aksClusterResourceId = \"/subscriptions/\u003csubscription-id\u003e/resourceGroups/\u003crg\u003e/providers/Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters/\u003ccluster-name\u003e\";\n\n            ContainerServiceFleetMemberData memberData = new ContainerServiceFleetMemberData\n            {\n                ClusterResourceId = new ResourceIdentifier(aksClusterResourceId)\n            };\n\n            ArmOperation\u003cContainerServiceFleetMemberResource\u003e operation = await members.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\n                WaitUntil.Completed,\n                \"\u003cmember-name\u003e\",\n                memberData);\n\n            ContainerServiceFleetMemberResource member = operation.Value;\n            Console.WriteLine($\"Added member: {member.Data.Name}\");\n            #endregion\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        [SyncOnly]\n        [Ignore(\"Only verifying that the sample builds\")]\n        public async Task CreateUpdateRun()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:CreateUpdateRun\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\n\n            ResourceIdentifier fleetId = ContainerServiceFleetResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(\n                \"\u003csubscription-id\u003e\",\n                \"\u003cresource-group-name\u003e\",\n                \"\u003cfleet-name\u003e\");\n\n            ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = client.GetContainerServiceFleetResource(fleetId);\n\n            ContainerServiceFleetUpdateRunData updateRunData = new ContainerServiceFleetUpdateRunData\n            {\n                ManagedClusterUpdate = new ContainerServiceFleetManagedClusterUpdate(\n                    new ContainerServiceFleetManagedClusterUpgradeSpec(\n                        ContainerServiceFleetManagedClusterUpgradeType.Full)\n                    {\n                        KubernetesVersion = \"1.30\"\n                    })\n            };\n\n            ArmOperation\u003cContainerServiceFleetUpdateRunResource\u003e operation = await fleet\n                .GetContainerServiceFleetUpdateRuns()\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \"\u003cupdate-run-name\u003e\", updateRunData);\n\n            ContainerServiceFleetUpdateRunResource updateRun = operation.Value;\n            Console.WriteLine($\"Created update run: {updateRun.Data.Name}\");\n            #endregion\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        [SyncOnly]\n        [Ignore(\"Only verifying that the sample builds\")]\n        public async Task CreateGate()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:CreateGate\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\n\n            ResourceIdentifier fleetId = ContainerServiceFleetResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(\n                \"\u003csubscription-id\u003e\",\n                \"\u003cresource-group-name\u003e\",\n                \"\u003cfleet-name\u003e\");\n\n            ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = client.GetContainerServiceFleetResource(fleetId);\n\n            ContainerServiceFleetGateData gateData = new ContainerServiceFleetGateData();\n\n            ArmOperation\u003cContainerServiceFleetGateResource\u003e operation = await fleet\n                .GetContainerServiceFleetGates()\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \"\u003cgate-name\u003e\", gateData);\n\n            ContainerServiceFleetGateResource gate = operation.Value;\n            Console.WriteLine($\"Created gate: {gate.Data.Name}\");\n            #endregion\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        [SyncOnly]\n        [Ignore(\"Only verifying that the sample builds\")]\n        public async Task CreateManagedNamespace()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:CreateManagedNamespace\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\n\n            ResourceIdentifier fleetId = ContainerServiceFleetResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(\n                \"\u003csubscription-id\u003e\",\n                \"\u003cresource-group-name\u003e\",\n                \"\u003cfleet-name\u003e\");\n\n            ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = client.GetContainerServiceFleetResource(fleetId);\n\n            ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData nsData = new ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData();\n\n            ArmOperation\u003cContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource\u003e operation = await fleet\n                .GetContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaces()\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \"\u003cnamespace-name\u003e\", nsData);\n\n            ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource managedNamespace = operation.Value;\n            Console.WriteLine($\"Created managed namespace: {managedNamespace.Data.Name}\");\n            #endregion\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe README at `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/README.md` currently has all required top-level 2026-04-07T07:04:04.1929789Z server:unified callBackendTool: serverID=safeoutputs, toolName=create_issue, args=map[body:## Documentation Gap
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1929795Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1929958Z **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet`
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1930165Z **Service directory:** `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/`
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1930260Z **Triggered by:** PR #57844 by @frantran
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1930319Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1930395Z ## What Changed
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1930399Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1930777Z PR #57844 generated version `1.2.0-beta.2` by upgrading the API version to `2025-08-01-preview`. This added two new resource types to the SDK:
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1930782Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1931379Z - **`ContainerServiceFleetGate` / `ContainerServiceFleetGateData` / `ContainerServiceFleetGateCollection`** — Gates support for fleet-level upgrade coordination
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1932046Z - **`ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespace` / `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData` / `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceCollection`** — Managed Namespace support
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1932052Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1932616Z Combined with features from `1.2.0-beta.1` (AutoUpgrade profiles, FleetMember labels, TargetKubernetesVersion), the package now has substantially more API surface than what the README describes.
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1932621Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1932693Z ## Gaps Found
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1932704Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1933763Z - The README **Examples** section contains no inline code samples — it only links to `https://aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples`, which is a generic redirect. There is no package-specific example showing how to create a Fleet, add a member, trigger an update run, create a Gate, or manage a ManagedNamespace.
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1934071Z - There are **no snippet placeholders** (`C# Snippet:`) in the README that would be populated by `Update-Snippets.ps1`.
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1934625Z - There is **no `tests/Samples/` directory** with hand-crafted sample test files. The only samples are the auto-generated ones under `tests/Generated/Samples/`, which are not wired into the README.
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1935304Z - The **Key concepts** section is a one-liner pointing to general SDK docs and says nothing specific to the Fleet service (e.g., what a Fleet is, what Members are, what Update Runs are, what Gates are, what Managed Namespaces are).
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1935689Z - The new **Gates** and **ManagedNamespace** features added in this and the prior beta are not mentioned or demonstrated anywhere in the README.
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1935694Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1935763Z <details>
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1935969Z <summary><strong>📐 Implementation Guide</strong></summary>
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1935975Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1936266Z This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1936276Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1936372Z ### Step 1: Update `README.md`
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1936377Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1936577Z **File:** `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/README.md`
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1936658Z **Action:** Edit
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1936663Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1936949Z Replace the placeholder **Key concepts** section with a concise description of the Fleet service domain model:
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1936954Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1937027Z ```markdown
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1937102Z ## Key concepts
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1937106Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1937179Z ### Fleet
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1937183Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1937673Z A `Fleet` resource (represented by `ContainerServiceFleetResource`) is the top-level management object that groups AKS member clusters for coordinated lifecycle management.
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1937678Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1937754Z ### Fleet Member
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1937758Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1938129Z A `ContainerServiceFleetMember` represents an AKS cluster enrolled in the fleet. Members can be labeled for targeted update strategies.
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1938200Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1938276Z ### Update Run
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1938280Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1938613Z A `ContainerServiceFleetUpdateRun` orchestrates rolling upgrades across member clusters in defined stages and groups.
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1938618Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1938704Z ### Update Strategy
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1938708Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1938982Z A `FleetUpdateStrategy` defines a reusable template of stages and groups for update runs.
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1938987Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1939077Z ### Auto Upgrade Profile
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1939082Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1939397Z An `AutoUpgradeProfile` automates Kubernetes version upgrades across fleet members based on release channel settings.
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1939402Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1939474Z ### Gate
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1939478Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1939989Z A `ContainerServiceFleetGate` is a pre/post-upgrade checkpoint that can pause an update run until external conditions are satisfied, enabling integration with deployment pipelines.
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1939998Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1940081Z ### Managed Namespace
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1940086Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1940606Z A `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespace` propagates a Kubernetes namespace (with RBAC, resource quotas, and network policies) across all fleet member clusters from the fleet hub.
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1940679Z ```
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1940683Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1940921Z Replace the placeholder **Examples** section content with snippet placeholders (see Step 2):
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1940926Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1941001Z ```markdown
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1941068Z ## Examples
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1941073Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1941347Z The following examples demonstrate common operations with the Azure Kubernetes Fleet Manager library.
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1941351Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1941423Z ### Create a Fleet
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1941431Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1941520Z ```C# Snippet:CreateFleet
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1941582Z ```

2026-04-07T07:04:04.1942929Z ```
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1942933Z 
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1943395Z > **Note:** The backtick fences above are abbreviated for clarity. In the actual README, use triple backticks on each opening and closing line as normal markdown code fences.
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1943400Z

2026-04-07T07:04:04.1995736Z ##[group]Run # Fix permissions on firewall logs so they can be uploaded as artifacts
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1996011Z [36;1m# Fix permissions on firewall logs so they can be uploaded as artifacts[0m
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1996141Z [36;1m# AWF runs with sudo, creating files owned by root[0m
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1996338Z [36;1msudo chmod -R a+r /tmp/gh-aw/sandbox/firewall/logs 2>/dev/null || true[0m
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1996645Z [36;1m# Only run awf logs summary if awf command exists (it may not be installed if workflow failed before install step)[0m
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1996853Z [36;1mif command -v awf &> /dev/null; then[0m
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1996979Z [36;1m  awf logs summary | tee -a "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"[0m
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1997053Z [36;1melse[0m
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1997217Z [36;1m  echo 'AWF binary not installed, skipping firewall log summary'[0m
2026-04-07T07:04:04.1997289Z [36;1mfi[0m
